data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-05-07T01:40:02.612Z</current_datetime>

I'm migrating a .NET 10 web API to .NET 11. The app uses EF Core with the Azure Cosmos DB provider.
We have a repository pattern where some methods use synchronous LINQ:

```csharp
public class ProductRepository
{
    private readonly CosmosDbContext _context;

    public List<Product> GetAll()
    {
        return _context.Products.ToList();
    }

    public Product GetById(int id)
    {
        return _context.Products.First(p => p.Id == id);
    }

    public int GetCount()
    {
        return _context.Products.Count();
    }

    public void Save(Product product)
    {
        _context.Products.Add(product);
        _context.SaveChanges();
    }
}
```

In .NET 10 we had the sync-over-async opt-in configured. What breaks in .NET 11?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about what breaks when migrating from .NET 10 to .NET 11 with EF Core using the Azure Cosmos DB provider, specifically around synchronous LINQ operations.

This is about a known breaking change in EF Core. Let me recall what I know:

In EF Core with the Azure Cosmos DB provider, synchronous operations were never natively supported - they were implemented as sync-over-async (blocking on async calls). 

In EF Core 9 (.NET 9), the Cosmos DB provider started throwing by default for sync operations, but you could opt in to allow sync-over-async behavior.


In .NET 10, that opt-in remained available but was marked as deprecated. By .NET 11, the sync-over-async workaround is completely gone—any synchronous LINQ call like `ToList()`, `First()`, or `Count()` will throw an `InvalidOperationException`. The migration path requires converting all these operations to their async equivalents. So in .NET 11, the sync-over-async opt-in will be removed entirely, meaning all synchronous LINQ operations like `ToList()`, `First()`, and `Count()` along with `SaveChanges()` will throw `InvalidOperationException` since Cosmos DB only supports async natively. I should explain what needs to change and how to migrate to async calls.
